samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits. Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits. Set to 8 to represent an 8-bit multiplier. Set to 7 to represent the number of bits minus one for an 8-bit multiplier. Register to store the value of mul_en_in for the next clock cycle. mul_en_in mul_en_out_reg assign Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled. mul_en_out_reg[-1] mul_en_out assign Register to store the value of mul_a for the next clock cycle. mul_a mul_a_reg assign Register to store the value of mul_b for the next clock cycle. mul_b mul_b_reg assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[0]}} temp[0] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[1]}} temp[1] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[2]}} temp[2] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[3]}} temp[3] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[4]}} temp[4] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[5]}} temp[5] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[6]}} temp[6] assign Generate partial products by performing bitwise operations between the multiplicand and the individual bits of the multiplier. mul_a_reg & {8{mul_b_reg[7]}} temp[7] assign Register to store the value of the first partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[0] sum[0] assign Register to store the value of the second partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[1] sum[1] assign Register to store the value of the third partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[2] sum[2] assign Register to store the value of the fourth partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[3] sum[3] assign Register to store the value of the fifth partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[4] sum[4] assign Register to store the value of the sixth partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[5] sum[5] assign Register to store the value of the seventh partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[6] sum[6] assign Register to store the value of the eighth partial product for the next clock cycle. temp[7] sum[7] assign Register to store the accumulated sum of all partial sums for the next clock cycle. sum[0] + sum[1] + sum[2] + sum[3] + sum[4] + sum[5] + sum[6] + sum[7] mul_out_reg assign Output signal representing the product of the multiplicand and multiplier. mul_out_reg mul_out assign

Implement the design of unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing.

It utilizes a clock signal clk, an active-low reset signal rst_n, an input enable signal mul_en_in, and provides an output enable signal mul_en_out and the product output mul_out of size 16 bits.

Module name: multi_pipe_8bit

Input ports:

  • clk
